variants.variant: Given that point $P$ is any point on the circle $x^{2}+y^{2}=49$, a vertical line is drawn from [BLANK] to the $x$-axis, with the foot of the perpendicular being $H$. If $ \overrightarrow{MH}= \frac {2}{3} \overrightarrow{PH}$, and the locus of $M$ is curve $E$. (1) Find the equation of $h(x)=f(x)-g(x)$; (2) Suppose two chords $MN$ and $PQ$ pass through the left focus of curve $E$, with the slopes of the lines containing $MN$ and $PQ$ being $k_{1}$ and $k_{2}$ respectively. Determine whether $ \frac {1}{|MN|}+ \frac {1}{|PQ|}$ is a constant value. If it is, find this value; if not, explain why.

Incomplete Problem:  
Considering point $P$ as any location on the circle defined by $x^{2}+y^{2}=16$, a vertical line is extended from [BLANK] to intersect the $x$-axis, where the foot of the perpendicular is denoted as $H$. If it holds that $ \overrightarrow{MH}= \frac {5}{8} \overrightarrow{PH}$, then the path traced by $M$ corresponds to curve $E$.

(1) Derive the equation of $h(x)=f(x)-g(x)$;

(2) Assume two chords $MN$ and $PQ$ intersect at the left focus of curve $E$, with the slopes of the lines that contain $MN$ and $PQ$ being $k_{1}$ and $k_{2}$ respectively. Ascertain whether $ \frac {1}{|MN|}+ \frac {1}{|PQ|}$ remains a constant. If so, calculate this constant; if not, provide a rationale.
